output_debit_classifier.reason: This is a standard accrual transaction. The business has consumed utility services worth $50 but has not yet paid for them. Under IFRS accrual accounting principles, expenses must be recognized in the period they are incurred, regardless of when cash is paid. The debit to Utilities expense records the cost of the service consumed during the period. The credit to Accrued liabilities establishes the obligation to pay the utility provider in the future. No tax is mentioned in the transaction, so no tax treatment is applied. The entry balances at $50 debit = $50 credit.

output_entry_builder.reason: The transaction accrues a $50 utility bill. Under the accrual basis of accounting (IAS 1), expenses are recognized when incurred, not when paid. The term 'accrue' indicates the utility service has been consumed but not yet paid for. This creates an expense (utilities expense) and a corresponding liability (accrued liabilities). The entry debits Utilities expense $50 to recognize the cost of the service consumed, and credits Accrued liabilities $50 to record the obligation to pay in the future.
